name: bluewalker
summary:
Bluewalker is a proof-of-concept Bluetooth LE scanner and advertiser which uses
_HCI user channel_ raw HCI sockets available on Linux to scan for Bluetooth LE
devices or to start advertising with user-specified data. User channel sockets
allow raw access to the Bluetooth controller and bypasses the Linux Bluetooth
stack. This allows Bluewalker access to all Bluetooth LE Advertisement Data sent
by nearby Bluetooth LE devices.

documentation.readme: Bluewalker is a proof-of-concept Bluetooth LE scanner and advertiser which uses HCI user channel raw HCI sockets available on Linux to scan for Bluetooth LE devices or to start advertising with user-specified data. User channel sockets allow raw access to the Bluetooth controller and bypasses the Linux Bluetooth stack. This allows Bluewalker access to all Bluetooth LE Advertisement Data sent by nearby Bluetooth LE devices.
usage_sections: In Collector mode it listens for advertising packets
In Observer mode it prints information about received
Ruuvi mode is special mode for listening and
Mijia mode is special mode for listening and
To display information only about devices with given address, use

arguments.name: -active
arguments.description: Active scanning

arguments.name: -broadcast
arguments.description: Send advertising data instead of scanning for it

arguments.name: -debug
arguments.description: Enable debug messages

arguments.name: -json
arguments.description: Output data as json

arguments.name: -log-trace
arguments.description: Enable more verbose trace logging in addition to debugging

arguments.name: -mijia
arguments.description: Scan and display information about Xiaomi Mijia devices

arguments.name: -observer
arguments.description: Do scanning in observer mode (display advertising packets as they are received)

arguments.name: -ruuvi
arguments.description: Scan and display information about found Ruuvi tags

arguments.name: -version
arguments.description: Print version number of the program

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

devices or to start advertising with user-specified data. Use.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: go install github.com/graipher/bluewalker@latest. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `go install github.com/graipher/bluewalker@latest`. Further invocations from upstream: `./build.sh arm`; `Usage of ./bluewalker:`. Bluewalker is a proof-of-concept Bluetooth LE scanner and advertiser which uses HCI user channel raw HCI sockets available on Linux to scan for Bluetooth LE devices or to start advertising with user-specified data. User channel sockets allow raw access to the Bluetooth controller and bypasses the Linux Bluetooth stack.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases:
When you need Bluetooth, use bluewalker because Bluewalker is a proof-of-concept Bluetooth LE scanner and advertiser which uses
_HCI user channel_ raw HCI sockets available on Linux to scan for Bluetooth LE
d.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `go install github.com/graipher/bluewalker@latest` as documented upstream for bluewalker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./build.sh arm` as documented upstream for bluewalker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Usage of ./bluewalker:` as documented upstream for bluewalker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Advertising data to send on broadcast mode (Format: "<type>,<data>;<type>,<data>", all values hexadecimal)` as documented upstream for bluewalker.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair bluewalker with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bluewalker --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
